title: Bandwidth Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt rendered in BuilderView; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Bandwidth** is a CPaaS (Communications-Platform-as-a-Service) provider. This connector — `BandwidthConnector` (class constant `CONNECTOR_TYPE = "bandwidth"`) — wraps three first-party Bandwidth APIs under a single HTTP Basic credential pair:

| Surface | Base URL | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Messaging | `https://messaging.bandwidth.com/api/v2` | SMS / MMS send + receive, media list/upload/delete |
| Voice | `https://voice.bandwidth.com/api/v2` | Programmable outbound calls, recordings list + download |
| Numbers / Dashboard | `https://dashboard.bandwidth.com/api` | Application registry, phone-number orders |

The connector exposes 21 public async methods (every method from `implementation_plan.md` Section 5), normalises message + call payloads into `NormalizedDocument` (scoped by `tenant_id` via `id = f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), and routes Bandwidth event callbacks through `handle_webhook` → `process_callback` (HMAC-SHA256 verification with `hmac.compare_digest`) → per-event handlers (`_handle_message_received`, `_handle_bridge_complete`, …).

Who uses it: any tenant on the Shielva platform that needs programmable SMS, MMS, or voice calls — call centres, two-way SMS support flows, IVR-driven workflows, recording archival pipelines.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Provision a Bandwidth API user

1. Sign in to https://dashboard.bandwidth.com.
2. Navigate to **Account → API Credentials → Add new API user**.
3. Grant Messaging Read+Write, Voice Read+Write, Dashboard Read.
4. Copy the **username** and the **password** (Bandwidth shows the password only once at creation).
5. From **Account Settings**, copy the numeric **Account ID** (e.g. `5000123`).

### 2. Install the connector in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors**, choose **Bandwidth**. Fill in:

| Install field | Value |
|---|---|
| `account_id` (text, required) | Your numeric account id |
| `username` (text, required) | API user from step 1 |
| `password` (secret, required) | API password from step 1 |
| `webhook_secret` (secret, optional) | HMAC-SHA256 secret if you want signed callback verification |
| `timeout_s` (number, optional) | HTTP timeout in seconds — default 60 |

Click **Install**. The connector calls `install()` which validates the required keys are present (it does **not** call the API at this point) and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)` if all are filled.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` next. That probes `GET https://dashboard.bandwidth.com/api/accounts/{account_id}/applications?size=1` with HTTP Basic. A 2xx response → `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`. A 401 → `TOKEN_EXPIRED + OFFLINE`. A 403 → `INVALID_CREDENTIALS + UNHEALTHY`. A 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with warning logged.

### 4. First sync

`sync(since=None, full=False)` iterates `list_messages` then `list_calls` (cursor-paginated via RFC 5988 `Link` header), normalises each to a `NormalizedDocument` with id `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`, and calls `await self.ingest_batch(documents, kb_id=...)`. Returns a `SyncResult` with `documents_synced`, `documents_failed`, `documents_found`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Bandwidth uses **HTTP Basic Authentication** (RFC 7617) — `AUTH_TYPE = "basic"`. There is no OAuth flow, no token exchange, and no expiry cycle.

### Credential model

- `account_id` is a **URL path segment**, not a header — every request URL contains `/accounts/{account_id}/…` (Dashboard, Voice) or `/users/{account_id}/…` (Messaging).
- `username` and `password` are encoded into the `Authorization` header:
  ```
  Authorization: Basic base64(username:password)
  ```
- Built once at `BandwidthHTTPClient.__init__` (see `client/http_client.py::_basic_auth_header`).

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `account_id`, `username`, `password` are all non-empty in `self.config`. Does **not** call any Bandwidth endpoint. Returns `HEALTHY + CONNECTED` (gateway treats this as install success). |
| `authorize(...)` | **Not implemented** — Basic auth has no token exchange. The base `BaseConnector.authorize` no-op is inherited. |
| `health_check()` | Lightweight probe against Dashboard `/applications`. Maps 401/403/429 via `_STATUS_MAP`. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — there is no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. The `webhook_secret` (if provided) is the only sensitive value held in `self.config`. |

### Why HTTP Basic is fine here

Bandwidth issues per-user API credentials with permission scoping in the Dashboard. Per-tenant API users + role-based grants give the same isolation guarantees as OAuth scopes for this CPaaS use case. Credentials live in the gateway's encrypted store; rotation is handled by re-running install with new values.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Defined in `metadata/connector.json::install_fields` and read via `self.config.get(...)` at runtime — **never** hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `account_id` | string | yes | `connector.py::__init__` → `self.account_id` | URL path segment |
| `username` | string | yes | `connector.py::__init__` → `self.username` | Basic-auth username |
| `password` | secret | yes | `connector.py::__init__` → `self.password` | Basic-auth password |
| `webhook_secret` | secret | no | `connector.py::process_callback` → `self.config.get("webhook_secret")` | HMAC-SHA256 secret for callback verification |
| `timeout_s` | number | no | `connector.py::__init__` → `float(self.config.get("timeout_s", 60.0))` | Per-request httpx timeout |

### Class-level constants

Provider-wide values that never vary per tenant — hardcoded in `client/http_client.py`:

```python
MESSAGING_BASE_URL = "https://messaging.bandwidth.com/api/v2"
VOICE_BASE_URL     = "https://voice.bandwidth.com/api/v2"
DASHBOARD_BASE_URL = "https://dashboard.bandwidth.com/api"
```

In `connector.py`:

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["account_id", "username", "password"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE", "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED", "CONNECTED"),
}
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content:
21 public async methods. Each one is a standalone `async def` in `connector.py` (OCP — none folded into `sync()`). All HTTP delegated to `BandwidthHTTPClient.request(...)` (SOC).

Grouped by surface; full param + return docs in `metadata/connector.json::apis`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate config keys + initialise the HTTP client. Does NOT call the API.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probe `GET /accounts/{account_id}/applications?size=1`. Classify by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterate messages + calls, normalise to `NormalizedDocument`, call `ingest_batch`. Returns `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Messaging
children.content:
### `async send_message(payload: Dict) -> Dict`
`POST /users/{account_id}/messages` — body is a Bandwidth message payload (`applicationId`, `to[]`, `from`, `text`, optional `media[]`).

### `async get_message(message_id: str) -> Dict`
`GET /users/{account_id}/messages/{messageId}` — single message lookup.

### `async list_messages(*, page_token=None, from_date_time=None, to_date_time=None, limit=100) -> Dict`
`GET /users/{account_id}/messages` — returns `{"items": [...], "next_page_token": str | None}`. Cursor extracted from `Link` header via `helpers/utils.py::parse_link_header` + `extract_page_token`.

### `async list_media() -> List[Dict]`
`GET /users/{account_id}/media`.

### `async upload_media(media_id, content: bytes, content_type="application/octet-stream") -> Dict`
`PUT /users/{account_id}/media/{mediaId}` — raw binary body.

### `async delete_media(media_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /users/{account_id}/media/{mediaId}`.

children.title: Voice
children.content:
### `async create_call(payload: Dict) -> Dict`
`POST /accounts/{account_id}/calls` — body has `applicationId`, `to`, `from`, `answerUrl`.

### `async get_call(call_id) -> Dict`
`GET /accounts/{account_id}/calls/{callId}`.

### `async update_call(call_id, payload) -> Dict`
`POST /accounts/{account_id}/calls/{callId}` — change call state or redirect (`state`, `redirectUrl`).

### `async list_calls(*, page_token=None, min_start_time=None, max_start_time=None, limit=100) -> Dict`
`GET /accounts/{account_id}/calls` — cursor-paginated like `list_messages`.

### `async get_call_recordings(call_id) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /accounts/{account_id}/calls/{callId}/recordings`.

### `async download_recording(call_id, recording_id) -> bytes`
`GET /accounts/{account_id}/calls/{callId}/recordings/{recordingId}/media` — returns raw audio bytes. `Accept: audio/wav` header set.

children.title: Numbers / Dashboard
children.content:
### `async list_applications() -> List[Dict]`
`GET /accounts/{account_id}/applications?size=100`. Used by `install()` validation and `health_check()` as the canonical probe.

### `async list_phone_numbers(*, page=1, size=100) -> Dict`
`GET /accounts/{account_id}/orders` — page/size pagination (not cursor).

children.title: Webhook + Event Handlers
children.content:
BaseConnector lifecycle overrides (signatures match exactly).

### `async handle_webhook(payload, headers=None) -> Dict`
If `webhook_secret` configured → calls `process_callback` for signature verification (returns `{status: "error"}` on mismatch). Then routes by `payload["eventType"]` to private handlers:
- `message-received` → `_handle_message_received`
- `message-delivered` → `_handle_message_delivered`
- `message-failed` → `_handle_message_failed`
- `bridge-complete` → `_handle_bridge_complete`
- `recording-available` → `_handle_recording_available`
Unknown event → `{status: "ignored", event_type: ...}`.

### `async process_callback(payload, headers=None) -> Dict`
Computes `HMAC-SHA256(payload, webhook_secret)` and compares with `X-Callback-Signature` header via `hmac.compare_digest` (timing-safe). Returns `{verified: True, data: payload}` on success, `{verified: False, error: "signature_mismatch"}` otherwise. When no secret is configured → returns `{verified: True, unverified: True}` so the caller can decide policy.

### `async handle_event(event) -> Dict`
Idempotency-keyed event ack. Returns `{event_id, processed: True}`.

### `async batch_processor(items, **kwargs) -> Dict`
Iterates through events, catches per-item exceptions, returns `{processed: N, failed: N, errors: [...]}`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
BandwidthError
├── BandwidthAuthError       (401 / 403)
├── BandwidthBadRequestError (400)
├── BandwidthNotFoundError   (404)
├── BandwidthConflictError   (409)
├── BandwidthRateLimitError  (429 — carries retry_after_s)
└── BandwidthServerError     (5xx)
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately — not retryable |
| 429 | Sleep `Retry-After` seconds (server-provided), retry up to `max_retries=3` |
| 5xx | Exponential backoff `min(2 ** attempt, 8)`, retry up to `max_retries=3` |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` | Exponential backoff, retry up to `max_retries=3` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

In `health_check()` and `sync()`, exceptions are caught at the lifecycle boundary and mapped via `_classify_failure`:

```python
status_code → (ConnectorHealth, AuthStatus):
   401 → (OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
   403 → (UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
   429 → (DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)  — logged as warning
   other → (UNHEALTHY, FAILED)
```

The gateway sees a `ConnectorStatus` with a meaningful `health` + `auth_status` rather than an unhandled stacktrace. `BandwidthAuthError` is treated as 403 (UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS) by `_classify_failure`.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | One of `account_id`, `username`, `password` is blank | Re-enter all three in ACP. Note: the password is shown only once at API-user creation in Bandwidth Dashboard — if you lost it, rotate the API user. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Bandwidth — API user lacks Dashboard Read | Grant Dashboard Read in **Account → API Credentials → Permissions**. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 from Bandwidth — bad password or API user revoked | Re-verify the username + password by signing into the Bandwidth dashboard. |
| Repeated `bandwidth.http.rate_limited` warnings | Hitting 429 (Bandwidth's per-account quota) | The client honours `Retry-After`, but if 429s persist, split traffic across multiple Bandwidth applications or lower call frequency. |
| Webhooks land but `process_callback` returns `verified: False` | `webhook_secret` mismatch with the Callback HMAC secret in Bandwidth dashboard | Re-enter the exact same secret in both places (no leading/trailing whitespace). |
| `download_recording` returns empty bytes | Recording not ready yet (Bandwidth needs ~30s post-call) | Listen for `recording-available` webhook event before downloading. |
| `list_messages` / `list_calls` infinite loop in test | Mock returned a next-token but real Bandwidth wouldn't | Default list mock must omit the next-token — test pagination explicitly via `side_effect` with two-page sequence (see `tests/test_connector.py::test_list_messages_default_no_next_token`). |
| `from connector import BandwidthConnector` fails in test | conftest.py missing `sys.path.insert(0, parent)` | Already fixed — `tests/conftest.py:17` inserts the package root onto sys.path. |
